36	# Lean Auditor
37	
38	You read **every `.lean` file in the project** and produce a per-file checklist of outdated comments, suspect definitions, dead-end proofs, and bad Lean practices. You are **read-only** and your only writable target is your own report under `task_results/`.

41	
42	## Stance: no strategic bias
43	
44	You audit the Lean **as Lean**. Your directive will NOT include the project strategy, the blueprint, or "what we are trying to prove" — and that is intentional. The point is to detect mathematically-wrong definitions and dead-end proofs *without* being swayed by what the project assumes should be true. A definition is wrong if it is wrong by reading the Lean alone (e.g. its body contradicts standard mathematical usage of the named concept); you do not need to know the strategy to spot that.
45	
46	This stance is also what makes you **critical of excuse-comments**. Lean-side comments like:
47	
48	- `-- We decided to use a temporary wrong definition; will fix it later.`
49	- `-- This proof is incomplete but works for now.`
50	- `-- TODO: replace before merging.`
51	- `-- Stand-in definition until we figure out the right one.`
52	
53	are RED FLAGS, not project workflow. Authors writing such comments are admitting the code is wrong; the comments cannot be allowed to silence the alarm. Flag every such comment with severity **major** at minimum — and **critical** when the named declaration is load-bearing.

80	   - Read the whole file.
81	   - For each declaration: is the signature reasonable as Lean? Is the body suspect (uses `axiom`, `Classical.choice _`, `sorry` with a body that looks like fake content, `:= True`, `:= rfl` on a non-trivial claim)?
82	   - For each comment: is it stale (referencing removed code or old strategies)? Is it an *excuse-comment* (see above)? Is it accurate about the code below it?
83	   - For each proof: is the tactic chain reasonable for the goal type, or does it look like it is going in the wrong direction (e.g. unfolding to manipulate syntax instead of using the available structure lemmas)?
84	   - Note any bad Lean practices: misuse of universe polymorphism, redundant typeclasses, deprecated tactics, anti-patterns like proving `False`-style lemmas to bypass infrastructure.

132	- **Excuse-comments** on any declaration: `-- TODO replace with real def`, `-- placeholder`, `-- temporary`, `-- wrong but works`, `-- will fix later`. These are admissions that the code is wrong; treat them as wrong.

1	# Archon Project

3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.

5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.

17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.

22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. Supports DeepSeek, Kimi (OpenAI-compatible via `--provider kimi`), Kimi (Anthropic-compatible via `--provider kimi-anthropic`), OpenRouter, OpenAI, and Gemini; defaults to `--provider auto` which picks the best available key automatically. **Requires at least one key in env** (`DEEPSEEK_API_KEY` / `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY`) — check via `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. Both `kimi` and `kimi-anthropic` use the same `MOONSHOT_API_KEY`; the latter speaks Moonshot's Anthropic-compatible Messages endpoint and supports `--think`. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)

50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.

71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.

91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. 146	/-! ## §1. The substrate tensor-product operation -/
147	
148	/-- **The substrate operation `⊗` on `Scheme.Modules X`.**
149	
150	For a scheme `X` and `M, N : X.Modules`, the tensor product
151	`M ⊗_X N : X.Modules` is the sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor
152	product `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` of the underlying presheaves of
153	`M` and `N` (affine-locally `(M ⊗_X N)(Spec A) = M(Spec A) ⊗_A N(Spec A)`).
154	
155	Per blueprint `def:scheme_modules_tensorobj`. The body lifts
156	`PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` through the sheafification functor on
157	the small Zariski site of `X` (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
158	noncomputable def tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
159	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
160	      (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf) M.val N.val) :
161	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
162	
163	/-- **Functoriality of `⊗_X`.**
164	
165	A pair of morphisms `f : M ⟶ M'` and `g : N ⟶ N'` in `X.Modules` determines a
166	morphism `f ⊗ g : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N'`, compatible with identities
167	and composition; the assignment `(M, N) ↦ tensorObj M N` thereby extends to a
168	bifunctor `X.Modules × X.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` natural in both arguments.
169	
170	Per blueprint `lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality`. The body inherits
171	the morphism action from `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` under
172	sheafification (fully defined, no `sorry`). -/
173	noncomputable def tensorObj_functoriality {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
174	    (f : M ⟶ M') (g : N ⟶ N') : tensorObj M N ⟶ tensorObj M' N' :=
175	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).map
176	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorHom
177	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)) f.val g.val)
178	
179	/-- **`⊗`-invertibility of an `𝒪_X`-module.** (Blueprint
180	`def:scheme_modules_isinvertible`.) `M : X.Modules` is `⊗`-invertible when it
181	admits a tensor inverse: an object `N` with `M ⊗_X N ≅ 𝒪_X`, where
182	`𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the designated unit. This is the
183	scheme-level analogue of Mathlib's `Module.Invertible`; the predicate carries its
184	inverse witness existentially, so the dual needed by the relative Picard group
185	law is definitional. -/
186	def IsInvertible {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : Prop :=
187	  ∃ N : X.Modules, Nonempty (tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
188	
189	/-- **The sheaf-level dual `M^∨ := ℋom_{𝒪_X}(M, 𝒪_X)`** of an `𝒪_X`-module.
190	
191	For a scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, the dual `dual M : X.Modules` is the
192	sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules dual `PresheafOfModules.dual` of the
193	underlying presheaf of `M` (the internal hom into the structure presheaf,
194	`M^∨(U) = ℋom_{𝒪_X|_U}(M|_U, 𝒪_X|_U)`).
195	
196	Construction = the **exact dual analogue of `tensorObj`** (this file, `tensorObj`):
197	apply the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 …)` on the
198	small Zariski site of `X` to the (axiom-clean, sub-step-3) presheaf dual
199	`PresheafOfModules.dual M.val`. The scheme's structure presheaf `X.presheaf` is
200	`CommRingCat`-valued over the single-universe topological site `Opens X`, hence is
201	exactly the base `R₀ : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat.{u}` that `PresheafOfModules.dual`
202	requires (the value `M^∨(U) = M|_U ⟶ R|_U` is an `R(U)`-module, needing
203	commutativity) — no CommRingCat/RingCat re-bridging is needed, since
204	`tensorObj` already takes `(R := X.presheaf)` over the same CommRingCat presheaf
205	and `X.ringCatSheaf.val = X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat` definitionally.
206	
207	The sheafification functor already lands in `SheafOfModules`, so no manual
208	`Presheaf.IsSheaf` / sheaf-condition descent is needed (sheafifying an already-sheaf
209	gives an iso object; this is the file's convention, matching `tensorObj`).
210	
211	Per blueprint `lem:internal_hom_isSheaf` (§`sec:tensorobj_dual_infra`); Stacks
212	tags 01CM (internal hom into a sheaf is a sheaf) / 01CR item 2. This is the
213	`⊗`-inverse candidate of an invertible sheaf, feeding `exists_tensorObj_inverse`. -/
214	noncomputable def dual {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
215	  ((PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).obj
216	      (PresheafOfModules.dual (R₀ := X.presheaf) M.val) :
217	    SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf)
218	
219	/-- **The sheaf-level dual is contravariantly functorial in isomorphisms.** An isomorphism
220	`e : M ≅ M'` in `X.Modules` induces `dual M' ≅ dual M`, obtained by sheafifying the presheaf-level
221	dual iso `PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso` of the underlying presheaf isomorphism. This is the
222	reusable "dual respects isos" ingredient (the dual analogue of `tensorObjIsoOfIso`) feeding the
223	assembly of `dual_isLocallyTrivial`: a trivialisation `L.restrict f ≅ 𝒪` yields, contravariantly,
224	`dual 𝒪 ≅ dual (L.restrict f)`. -/
225	noncomputable def dualIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules} (e : M ≅ M') :
226	    dual M' ≅ dual M :=
227	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
228	    (PresheafOfModules.dualIsoOfIso (R₀ := X.presheaf)
229	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e))
230	
231	/-! ## §2. (Off the critical path) the full monoidal-category structure
232	
233	iter-206 PIVOT: the full `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance is
234	**deliberately not built**. Per blueprint `rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path`,
235	the relative Picard group law is a structure on *isomorphism classes* of line
236	bundles — every group axiom is a `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` proposition, so no monoidal
237	coherence (pentagon/triangle/hexagon) and no `MonoidalClosed` structure is ever
238	consumed. The earlier `monoidalCategory := sorry` instance (and the two
239	localization-transport supplements `isMonoidal_W_of_whiskerLeft`,
240	`monoidalCategoryOfIsMonoidalW`) routed through the verified-absent
241	`MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R₀)` wall; they are removed here. The group
242	law is built directly on the line-bundle subcategory from the four
243	existence-of-iso lemmas below, mirroring Mathlib's `CommRing.Pic`. -/
244	
245	/-! ## §3. The lift through `LineBundle.OnProduct` (PUSH-BEYOND supporting lemmas)
246	
247	The following helper lemmas record the lift of the substrate to the
248	locally-trivial subcategory used by the relative Picard consumer. They are not
249	`\lean{...}`-pinned in the blueprint (their statements are descriptive); the
250	typed signatures here scaffold the iter-203+ bodies. -/
251	
252	/-- **The substrate operation respects isomorphisms in both arguments.**
253	
254	A pair of isomorphisms `e : M ≅ M'` and `e' : N ≅ N'` in `X.Modules` induces an
255	isomorphism `tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N'`, obtained by sheafifying the
256	tensor product (in the presheaf-of-modules monoidal category) of the underlying
257	presheaf isomorphisms `e.val`, `e'.val`. Its `hom` is
258	`tensorObj_functoriality e.hom e'.hom`. This is the reusable functor-of-isos
259	ingredient feeding `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`. -/
260	noncomputable def tensorObjIsoOfIso {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' N N' : X.Modules}
261	    (e : M ≅ M') (e' : N ≅ N') : tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj M' N' :=
262	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
263	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorIso
264	      (C := _root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat))
265	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e)
266	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).mapIso e'))
267	
268	/-- **The substrate tensor of the structure sheaf with itself is the structure
269	sheaf.**
270	
271	`tensorObj 𝒪_X 𝒪_X ≅ 𝒪_X`, where `𝒪_X = SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf` is the
272	designated unit object. Built from the presheaf-level left unitor
273	`λ_ (𝟙_)` (the unit of the `PresheafOfModules` monoidal category is exactly
274	`SheafOfModules.unit.val`) under sheafification, composed with the
275	sheafification-adjunction counit isomorphism on the (already-sheaf) unit. -/
276	noncomputable def tensorObj_unit_iso {X : Scheme.{u}} :
277	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
278	      ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
279	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
280	      (λ_ (𝟙_ (_root_.PresheafOfModules (X.presheaf ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)))) ≪≫
281	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app
282	      (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
283	
284	/-- **Left unitor for `⊗_X`.** `𝒪_X ⊗_X M ≅ M`. (Blueprint
285	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, left half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor`.)
286	Sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor `λ_ M.val`, composed with the
287	sheafification counit identifying `sheafification M.val` with the (already-sheaf)
288	`M`. The cheap `mapIso` pattern; uses no abstract pullback. -/
289	noncomputable def tensorObj_left_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
290	    tensorObj (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) M ≅ M :=
291	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
292	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).leftUnitor M.val) ≪≫
293	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
294	
295	/-- **Right unitor for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X 𝒪_X ≅ M`. (Blueprint
296	`lem:tensorobj_unit_iso`, right half, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor`.)
297	Sheafification of the presheaf-level right unitor `ρ_ M.val`, composed with the
298	sheafification counit. -/
299	noncomputable def tensorObj_right_unitor {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) :
300	    tensorObj M (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≅ M :=
301	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification (R := X.ringCatSheaf) (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).mapIso
302	      ((PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategoryStruct (R := X.presheaf)).rightUnitor M.val) ≪≫
303	    (asIso (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.val)).counit).app M
304	
305	/-- **Braiding for `⊗_X`.** `M ⊗_X N ≅ N ⊗_X M`. (Blueprint
306	`lem:tensorobj_comm_iso`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_braiding`.)
307	The presheaf-of-modules monoidal category is symmetric; its braiding `β_ M.val
308	N.val` sheafifies to the asserted isomorphism by the cheap `mapIso` pattern. -/

310	    tensorObj M N ≅ tensorObj N M :=

399	    CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat _

401	  refine (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback W.ι).app M ≪≫ ?_
402	  -- ≅ (pullback (j ≫ U.ι)).obj M
403	  refine (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr hjι.symm).app M ≪≫ ?_
404	  -- ≅ (pullback j).obj ((pullback U.ι).obj M)

569	  have hst : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
570	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
571	    intro x

573	      have hmem : x ∈ (U x).ι.opensRange := by
574	        rw [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]; exact hxU x

576	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map φ) := h x
577	    -- `(restrictFunctor … ⋙ toPresheaf … ⋙ stalkFunctor x').map φ` is iso (functor of an iso).

580	      dsimp only [Functor.comp_map]; exact Functor.map_isIso _ _

585	        (Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso (U x).ι x') φ).mp hFφ

589	  let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩

615	    (hg : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ) (r : X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj V) (m : M.val.obj V),

714	/-! ## §5. The invertibility-carrier Picard group `picCommGroup`
715	
716	This is the by-hand commutative-group law on isomorphism classes of `⊗`-invertible
717	`𝒪_X`-modules (blueprint §`sec:tensorobj_pic_carrier`). Every group axiom is a single
718	existence-of-isomorphism `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` read as an equality of iso-classes; no
719	pentagon/triangle/hexagon coherence and no `MonoidalCategory` instance is invoked.
720	The inverse is carried by the membership witness of `IsInvertible` itself. -/
721	
722	/-- **Step 1 — associator on `⊗`-invertible objects** (blueprint
723	`lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso_invertible`). An immediate specialisation of the now
724	*unconditional* `tensorObj_assoc_iso`; the invertibility hypotheses are not consumed
725	(they match the blueprint statement). -/
726	noncomputable def tensorObj_assoc_iso_invertible {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N P : X.Modules}
727	    (_hM : IsInvertible M) (_hN : IsInvertible N) (_hP : IsInvertible P) :
728	    tensorObj (tensorObj M N) P ≅ tensorObj M (tensorObj N P) :=
729	  tensorObj_assoc_iso
730	
731	/-- **Middle-four interchange for `⊗_X`** (helper). For arbitrary `𝒪_X`-modules
732	`A, B, C, D`, there is an isomorphism `(A ⊗ B) ⊗ (C ⊗ D) ≅ (A ⊗ C) ⊗ (B ⊗ D)`,
733	assembled from the unconditional associator and the braiding (no coherence consumed).
734	Used to reassociate the four factors in `IsInvertible.tensorObj`. -/
735	private noncomputable def tensorObj_middleFour {X : Scheme.{u}} (A B C D : X.Modules) :
736	    tensorObj (tensorObj A B) (tensorObj C D)
737	      ≅ tensorObj (tensorObj A C) (tensorObj B D) :=
738	  tensorObj_assoc_iso ≪≫
739	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl A)
740	      (tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm ≪≫
741	        tensorObjIsoOfIso (tensorObj_braiding B C) (Iso.refl D) ≪≫
742	        tensorObj_assoc_iso) ≪≫
743	    tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm
744	
745	/-- **Step 3 — `⊗`-invertibility is closed under tensor product** (blueprint
746	`lem:isinvertible_tensor`). If `M, M'` are `⊗`-invertible with inverses `N, N'`,
747	then `N ⊗ N'` is a tensor inverse of `M ⊗ M'`. -/
748	theorem IsInvertible.tensorObj {X : Scheme.{u}} {M M' : X.Modules}
749	    (hM : IsInvertible M) (hM' : IsInvertible M') :
750	    IsInvertible (Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M M') := by
751	  obtain ⟨N, ⟨e⟩⟩ := hM
752	  obtain ⟨N', ⟨e'⟩⟩ := hM'
753	  exact ⟨Scheme.Modules.tensorObj N N',
754	    ⟨tensorObj_middleFour M M' N N' ≪≫ tensorObjIsoOfIso e e' ≪≫ tensorObj_unit_iso⟩⟩
755	
756	/-- **Step 4 — the structure sheaf is `⊗`-invertible** (blueprint
757	`lem:isinvertible_unit`). Witness `𝒪_X`, iso `tensorObj_unit_iso`. -/
758	theorem isInvertible_unit {X : Scheme.{u}} :
759	    IsInvertible (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :=
760	  ⟨SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf, ⟨tensorObj_unit_iso⟩⟩
761	
762	/-- **Step 5 — the tensor inverse is determined up to isomorphism** (blueprint
763	`lem:isinvertible_inverse_welldef`). If `M ⊗ N ≅ 𝒪_X` and `M ⊗ N' ≅ 𝒪_X` then
764	`N ≅ N'`, via the inverse-of-inverse chain. -/
765	theorem IsInvertible.inverse_unique {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N N' : X.Modules}
766	    (e : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf)
767	    (e' : Scheme.Modules.tensorObj M N' ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :
768	    Nonempty (N ≅ N') :=
769	  ⟨(tensorObj_right_unitor N).symm ≪≫
770	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (Iso.refl N) e'.symm ≪≫
771	    tensorObj_assoc_iso.symm ≪≫
772	    tensorObjIsoOfIso (tensorObj_braiding N M ≪≫ e) (Iso.refl N') ≪≫
773	    tensorObj_left_unitor N'⟩
774	
775	/-- The setoid of `⊗`-invertible `𝒪_X`-modules: `M ∼ M'` iff there exists an
776	isomorphism `M ≅ M'` (blueprint `def:pic_carrier`). -/
777	instance picSetoid (X : Scheme.{u}) : Setoid {M : X.Modules // IsInvertible M} where
778	  r M M' := Nonempty ((M : X.Modules) ≅ (M' : X.Modules))
779	  iseqv :=
780	    ⟨fun _ => ⟨Iso.refl _⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ => ⟨e.symm⟩, fun ⟨e⟩ ⟨f⟩ => ⟨e ≪≫ f⟩⟩
781	
782	/-- **Step 2 — the invertibility-carrier Picard group** (blueprint
783	`def:pic_carrier`): the quotient of `⊗`-invertible `𝒪_X`-modules by isomorphism. -/
784	def PicGroup (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ := Quotient (picSetoid X)

1228	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — D1: the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition
1229	
1230	These are general `PresheafOfModules` statements (any `F : C ⥤ D`, ring presheaves
1231	`R, S`), not specific to schemes; they are project-local because Mathlib's pinned commit
1232	exposes neither `extendScalars` nor `pullback₀` at the presheaf-of-modules level. Both are
1233	realised as the left adjoint of an existing right adjoint: `pushforward₀ F R` is
1234	definitionally `pushforward (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))` (because `restrictScalars (𝟙) = 𝟭` on the nose,
1235	witnessed by Mathlib's `restrictScalars (𝟙 R)).Full := inferInstanceAs (𝟭 _).Full`), and
1236	`restrictScalars φ` is definitionally `pushforward (F := 𝟭) φ`; since `pushforward _` is
1237	always a right adjoint, so are these two, and their left adjoints `pullback₀`/`extendScalars`
1238	exist. The decomposition `pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback₀` then follows from the
1239	definitional factorisation `pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ` by
1240	uniqueness of left adjoints (`Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso`).
1241	
1242	Per blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition` (D1). **OFF-PATH (iter-243 pivot).** D1 is
1243	axiom-clean and self-contained, but it was the first brick of the ABANDONED general
1244	strong-monoidal pullback build (`sec:tensorobj_pullback_monoidality`, route (e)); the active
1245	route is the loc-triv chart-chase D1'–D4' (§below), which does NOT consume `extendScalars`/
1246	`pullback₀`. Retained as a correct, reusable presheaf-level decomposition; do NOT extend it
1247	toward the general build (D2 scalar-strong / D3 topological-interchange are NOT pursued). -/
1248	
1249	section PullbackLanDecomposition
1250	
1251	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{u} C] {D : Type u} [Category.{u} D]
1252	  {F : C ⥤ D} {R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}} {S : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}}
1253	
1254	/-- `pushforward₀ F R` is a right adjoint: it is definitionally `pushforward (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))`
1255	(since `restrictScalars (𝟙) = 𝟭` on the nose). Project-local; carries the existence of the
1256	topological inverse image `pullback₀`. -/
1257	private lemma pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1258	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀.{u} F R).IsRightAdjoint :=
1259	  inferInstanceAs (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (𝟙 (F.op ⋙ R))).IsRightAdjoint
1260	
1261	/-- `restrictScalars φ` is a right adjoint: it is definitionally `pushforward (F := 𝟭) φ`.
1262	Project-local; carries the existence of the extension of scalars `extendScalars`. -/
1263	private lemma restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1264	    (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars.{u} φ).IsRightAdjoint :=
1265	  inferInstanceAs
1266	    (PresheafOfModules.pushforward.{u} (F := 𝟭 C) (R := F.op ⋙ R) φ).IsRightAdjoint
1267	
1268	/-- **The topological inverse image `pullback₀ := (pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint`**, the
1269	left adjoint of the fixed-ring pushforward. On underlying presheaves it is the left Kan
1270	extension along `F.op`. Project-local (no `PresheafOfModules` inverse-image functor at the
1271	pin); the carrier of D3. -/
1272	noncomputable def pullback0 (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1273	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} R :=
1274	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1275	  (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R).leftAdjoint
1276	
1277	/-- **Extension of scalars `extendScalars φ := (restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint`**, the left
1278	adjoint of restriction of scalars along a morphism of ring presheaves. Project-local; the
1279	carrier of D2 (strong monoidal via `distribBaseChange`). -/
1280	noncomputable def extendScalars (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1281	    _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} S ⥤ _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (F.op ⋙ R) :=
1282	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1283	  (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ).leftAdjoint
1284	
1285	/-- The adjunction `pullback₀ ⊣ pushforward₀`. -/
1286	noncomputable def pullback0Adjunction (F : C ⥤ D) (R : Dᵒᵖ ⥤ RingCat.{u}) :
1287	    pullback0 F R ⊣ PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R :=
1288	  haveI := pushforward₀IsRightAdjoint F R
1289	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.pushforward₀ F R)
1290	
1291	/-- The adjunction `extendScalars φ ⊣ restrictScalars φ`. -/
1292	noncomputable def extendScalarsAdjunction (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1293	    extendScalars φ ⊣ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ :=
1294	  haveI := restrictScalarsIsRightAdjoint φ
1295	  Adjunction.ofIsRightAdjoint (PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars φ)
1296	
1297	/-- **D1 — the presheaf pullback Lan decomposition** (blueprint `lem:pullback_lan_decomposition`).
1298	For `φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R` presenting a pushforward of presheaves of modules, the presheaf
1299	pullback factors as extension of scalars followed by the topological inverse image,
1300	`pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback₀`. This is the left-adjoint reversal of the
1301	definitional factorisation `pushforward φ = pushforward₀ F R ⋙ restrictScalars φ`, obtained
1302	from `Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso` (uniqueness of left adjoints). Project-local. -/
1303	noncomputable def pullbackLanDecomposition (φ : S ⟶ F.op ⋙ R) :
1304	    PresheafOfModules.pullback φ ≅ extendScalars φ ⋙ pullback0 F R :=
1305	  (Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso
1306	    (extendScalarsAdjunction φ) (pullback0Adjunction F R)
1307	    (PresheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction φ)
1308	    (Iso.refl (PresheafOfModules.pushforward φ))).symm
1309	
1310	end PullbackLanDecomposition
